ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Ɛ na yee balaŋ baŋ baa tale ko deniwalaŋ kaageŋ bo, mɔna bɔɔ tale yɛɛ kolo kpu bela nɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ yee Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ko walaŋ taŋ na, ɔ dana doŋ nyi waa fuŋ e na ɔ woo pou ka do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.
translated: I am going to the village of the ancestors, but the path is long and the journey difficult. But I will go to Wurubuarɛ, even if I must walk through the forest and face all its trials.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ ɔ na wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Galelia boo nɔɔman. Te mena keŋ balaŋ burum be kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man gyu loŋ keŋ dɔɔ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.
translated: Now when Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew into Galilee. But he himself went into the wilderness, preaching, saying, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand." And there went out to him from Jerusalem and all Judea and all the region around the Jordan, and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido, na ke nyiŋ naa fa balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.
translated: The people said that Kanea went and did not return here, but he was unable to come back to do something at Adido, and when they knew how to go see the place where they had to make peace, they did so.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.

translated: Why did you go to the house? Are you sure if you know that the dog ran from the man, and you are with me?

translated: God summoned and spoke to Jesus, saying to him, "Simon has changed his name to Peter, and..."

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.
translated: Jesus said to them, "It is finished, for the one who believes in the Lord of the heavenly kingdom will have eternal life, and the one who believes in the Lord on earth will have salvation."

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ dɔŋ balaŋ siaman. Ɛ ne tale kyɔsee lese wonembia buruburu lee ɛ soloŋse man, mɔna ɛ ne lilii wonembia dinaana.
translated: He who sows in the field will reap in the harvest. And he who speaks lies will lose his honor, but he who keeps the truth will find glory.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ ŋon na maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ.
translated: Walaŋ has a problem that he did something, and he did something because Wurubuarɛ made him do it so that he could...

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
text: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ?
translated: Akpaa is the king of the place you are going to, do you speak English?

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.
translated: When Jesus was teaching, he said, "I tell you, many will enter through the narrow gate into Paradise, but few will enter through the wide gate into Paradise."

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɛɛ lee Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum sila wɔ.
translated: Jesus healed him when he came down to Jericho; the man saw his sins.

text: Ɔ dana ɔ pampaa ɔ nyiŋmaa man waa mo feefu wa amɔla na waa lese amɔla bia ŋan do ɔ kpee man, na waa kuu wura ŋan ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ Maake
translated: He came to the village and the people gathered around him, and he taught them many things and healed their sick compassionately. Jesus the Messiah said to Mark.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ nyiŋa ɔ wose.
translated: I spoke, and he did it with his hand, and he rested.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man balinɔɔ weese nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ kom ŋgba mena keŋ wɔɔte ŋon be yako nyi waa tɔ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.
translated: Balaŋ went to the river to fetch water but fell in, and the people tried to rescue him but could not because the current was too strong.
